target.name: Einride
target.founder: Robert Falck, Filip Lilja, Linnéa Kornehed

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $1.8B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 2 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Harbinger
private.rationale: Same sector (Transportation), same stage (Series C), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Einride, a Series C autonomous trucking company founded by Robert Falck, Filip Lilja, and Linnéa Kornehed, is valued at $1.8B post-money on $752M+ raised. The Transportation sector comprises 7 private peer companies (Rivian, Harbinger, The Boring Company, REGENT Craft, ZeroAvia, and others) and 0 public comparables, limiting traditional peer benchmarking.

Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.7x multiplier to the $1.8B Series C valuation, implying $3.1B at the next funding stage. This method captures typical venture step-up patterns for mature Series C rounds in deep-tech transportation. Peer-median analysis does not apply: while the Transportation sector includes four same-sector peers, only one trades at the same stage, and insufficient datapoints exist within narrow valuation tiers or adjacent stages to support a reliable median. Relying on broader peer medians across sector or stage would introduce unwarranted noise given the heterogeneous business models and development timelines of transportation frontier-tech companies.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $2.6B to $3.5B, with a median of $3.1B. This range reflects the stage-progression method's output and reasonable confidence bounds. Key uncertainties center on the selection of the step-up multiplier (which assumes Einride's next round will follow typical venture pacing) and the absence of comparable late-stage or public transportation peers to validate the implied multiple against market-observed pricing. The narrow methodology set reflects data constraints rather than valuation confidence.
